title: Perioperative Safety for a 68-Year-Old Scheduled for Right Total Knee Arthroplasty: Preventing Wrong-Site/Wrong-Patient/Wrong-Procedure Surgery and Retained Surgical Items Through the Universal Protocol (Verification, Site Marking, and the Team TIME-OUT), the WHO Surgical Safety Checklist, Surgical Counts, Sterile Technique, an SSI-Prevention Bundle, and a Speak-Up Team Culture
subtitle: WRONG-SITE, WRONG-PROCEDURE, and WRONG-PATIENT surgery — and RETAINED SURGICAL ITEMS (sponges/instruments/sharps left in the body) — are NEVER EVENTS (serious reportable, preventable sentinel events), and they are prevented by disciplined, team-based safety processes in which the NURSE is a central patient advocate. THE UNIVERSAL PROTOCOL (Joint Commission) has three components: (1) PRE-PROCEDURE VERIFICATION — confirm the CORRECT PATIENT (using at least TWO identifiers, e.g., name + date of birth, NOT room number), the CORRECT PROCEDURE, and the CORRECT SITE/SIDE; verify that the informed CONSENT matches the planned procedure and the patient's understanding, and that relevant documents, IMAGING, labs, blood products, and any IMPLANTS/special equipment are available; involve the patient ACTIVELY (awake patients confirm their own identity/procedure/site); and RECONCILE ANY DISCREPANCY (consent vs schedule vs patient statement vs imaging) BEFORE proceeding — never 'assume' or proceed through a mismatch. (2) MARK THE SITE — the operative site is MARKED by the licensed provider who will perform the procedure (ideally with the patient involved and awake), using an unambiguous, consistent mark (e.g., the surgeon's initials) AT or near the incision site, positioned to remain VISIBLE after skin prep and draping; mark ONLY the intended site (not non-operative sites), and required especially for laterality (right vs left), multiple structures (e.g., fingers/toes), and multiple levels (spine). (3) TIME-OUT — IMMEDIATELY BEFORE the incision/start of the procedure, the ENTIRE team STOPS all activity and actively, verbally CONFIRMS, at minimum: correct PATIENT (two identifiers), correct PROCEDURE, correct SITE/SIDE and position, consent, and the availability of correct implants/equipment/imaging, plus confirmation that prophylactic ANTIBIOTICS were given (if indicated) and any anticipated critical concerns — and EVERY team member participates and must agree before proceeding; the time-out is documented. THE WHO SURGICAL SAFETY CHECKLIST structures three pauses: SIGN IN (before induction of anesthesia — identity/site/procedure/consent, anesthesia safety check, allergies, airway/aspiration risk, blood loss risk), TIME OUT (before skin incision — team introductions, the verification above, antibiotic prophylaxis, imaging, anticipated critical events), and SIGN OUT (before the patient leaves the room — confirm the procedure recorded, that instrument/sponge/needle COUNTS are CORRECT, SPECIMENS are correctly LABELED, any equipment problems, and key recovery/management concerns). SPEAK-UP / 'STOP-THE-LINE' CULTURE: ANY team member — regardless of role or seniority — can and MUST halt the procedure if something does not match or seems unsafe; a flattened hierarchy and a just culture make this safe and expected (the circulating nurse is a key advocate empowered to stop the line). SURGICAL COUNTS to prevent RETAINED SURGICAL ITEMS: SPONGES, SHARPS (needles/blades), and INSTRUMENTS are COUNTED (by two people, typically the scrub and circulating nurse) BEFORE the procedure (baseline), before closure of a cavity/wound, at skin closure, and at any permanent relief of staff; if a COUNT IS INCORRECT, the team is notified, the surgeon is informed, the wound/field/floor/trash are searched, and an X-RAY is obtained to locate the item — the discrepancy must be RECONCILED (item found or accounted for) BEFORE the patient leaves the OR; counts are documented. STERILE TECHNIQUE / SURGICAL ASEPSIS: establish and maintain a STERILE FIELD — only STERILE items touch sterile items ('sterile-to-sterile'); the field is kept in constant view and NOT left unattended; do NOT reach OVER or turn your BACK on a sterile field; sterile gowns are sterile in front from chest to sterile-field level and the sleeves; the OUTER 1 INCH (2.5 cm) of a sterile drape/field edge is considered NON-sterile; MOISTURE causes 'strikethrough' contamination; and 'when in doubt, throw it out' (consider any questionable item contaminated). SURGICAL-SITE-INFECTION (SSI) PREVENTION BUNDLE: administer prophylactic ANTIBIOTICS within 60 MINUTES before incision (re-dose for long cases/major blood loss, and discontinue appropriately postoperatively); appropriate HAIR REMOVAL — do NOT shave with a razor (microabrasions increase SSI); use CLIPPERS or no removal; perform skin ANTISEPSIS (e.g., chlorhexidine-alcohol) and allow it to DRY (also a FIRE-safety point with alcohol preps + oxygen + cautery); maintain perioperative NORMOTHERMIA and GLUCOSE control; maintain sterile technique and appropriate oxygenation. OTHER perioperative-safety elements: label ALL medications/solutions on and off the sterile field; correctly handle/label SPECIMENS; FIRE safety (the fire triad — oxidizer/oxygen + ignition/cautery + fuel/alcohol prep, drapes); patient POSITIONING with pressure-injury and nerve-injury prevention; VTE prophylaxis; and correct equipment/implant verification. NURSING ROLE (perioperative/circulating RN as patient advocate): verify patient/procedure/site/consent with two identifiers and reconcile discrepancies; ensure site marking; LEAD/participate in the time-out and the WHO checklist with active team participation; perform and reconcile surgical COUNTS; establish and protect the sterile field/asepsis; ensure timely antibiotic prophylaxis and the SSI bundle; empower and use a SPEAK-UP culture (stop the line for any concern); manage specimens, fire safety, positioning; and document — preventing never events and surgical-site infection

patient.name: K. Olsen
patient.code: Full code; alert with capacity; consented for surgery; goal = the correct procedure on the correct site safely, with prevention of wrong-site surgery, retained items, and surgical-site infection
patient.admitDate: Scheduled for an elective RIGHT total knee arthroplasty for end-stage osteoarthritis. In the pre-op holding area and then the OR, the perioperative (circulating) nurse carries out the Universal Protocol and surgical-safety processes: verifying the correct patient/procedure/site against the consent (a near-miss is caught — the OR schedule briefly listed 'left knee' while the consent, the surgeon's plan, the imaging, and the patient all confirm the RIGHT knee), ensuring site marking by the surgeon, leading the team time-out, managing surgical counts, maintaining sterile technique, ensuring SSI-prevention measures, and empowering a speak-up culture.
patient.room: Pre-op holding → operating room; full surgical team (surgeon, anesthesia, scrub nurse/tech, circulating RN); WHO Surgical Safety Checklist + count board in use

labs.n: PRESENTATION + never events + the nurse as advocate
labs.v: 68 y/o woman K. Olsen scheduled for an elective RIGHT total knee arthroplasty for end-stage osteoarthritis. The perioperative (circulating) NURSE is a central patient advocate for SURGICAL SAFETY, charged with preventing NEVER EVENTS — WRONG-SITE, WRONG-PROCEDURE, and WRONG-PATIENT surgery, and RETAINED SURGICAL ITEMS (sponges/instruments/needles left in the body) — which are serious reportable, PREVENTABLE sentinel events, as well as preventing SURGICAL-SITE INFECTION (SSI). These harms are prevented not by luck but by disciplined, TEAM-BASED safety PROCESSES: the Universal Protocol (verification, site marking, time-out), the WHO Surgical Safety Checklist (sign-in/time-out/sign-out), surgical COUNTS, sterile technique, the SSI-prevention bundle, and a SPEAK-UP team culture. In this case, the nurse's pre-procedure verification CATCHES A NEAR-MISS: the OR schedule briefly listed 'left knee,' whereas the consent, the surgeon's operative plan, the imaging, and the awake patient all confirm the RIGHT knee — the discrepancy is flagged and RECONCILED before proceeding. PMH: osteoarthritis; otherwise stable; alert with capacity; consented. SOCIAL: supportive family in the waiting area. The nurse's role across pre-op, intra-op, and the hand-off: verify patient/procedure/site/consent (two identifiers), ensure site marking, lead/participate in the time-out and checklist, perform and reconcile counts, maintain the sterile field and asepsis, ensure SSI-prevention measures, empower speak-up/stop-the-line, manage fire safety/positioning/specimens, and document — preventing never events and infection. This is fundamentally a Leadership/teamwork + Safety competency.
labs.r: Wrong-site/procedure/patient surgery + retained surgical items are PREVENTABLE never events; SSI is preventable. They are prevented by team safety PROCESSES: Universal Protocol (verify/mark/time-out), WHO checklist, counts, sterile technique, SSI bundle, speak-up. The nurse is the patient advocate who catches discrepancies (here, a 'left knee' schedule vs 'right knee' everything-else near-miss). RN role: lead/participate in verification + safety processes.

labs.n: The Universal Protocol — verification, site marking, time-out
labs.v: THE UNIVERSAL PROTOCOL (Joint Commission) prevents wrong-site/procedure/patient surgery through three components: (1) PRE-PROCEDURE VERIFICATION — confirm the CORRECT PATIENT using at least TWO IDENTIFIERS (e.g., full name + date of birth or medical record number — NEVER the room/bed number), the CORRECT PROCEDURE, and the CORRECT SITE/SIDE; verify the signed informed CONSENT matches the planned procedure and the patient's stated understanding; confirm that needed DOCUMENTS, IMAGING, labs, blood products, and any IMPLANTS/special equipment are present; and involve the PATIENT actively (an awake, competent patient states their own name, the procedure, and the site/side). CRUCIALLY, RECONCILE ANY DISCREPANCY (consent vs schedule vs surgeon vs imaging vs patient statement) BEFORE proceeding — STOP and clarify; do NOT assume or proceed through a mismatch (this is exactly how the 'left vs right knee' near-miss is caught). (2) MARK THE SITE — the operative site is MARKED by the LICENSED INDEPENDENT PRACTITIONER who will perform the procedure (the surgeon), ideally with the patient INVOLVED and awake, using an UNAMBIGUOUS, consistent mark (commonly the surgeon's initials) AT or adjacent to the incision site and positioned to remain VISIBLE after skin prep and draping; mark ONLY the operative site (do NOT mark non-operative sites), and marking is especially required for LATERALITY (right/left), MULTIPLE structures (fingers, toes), and MULTIPLE LEVELS (spine). (3) TIME-OUT — IMMEDIATELY BEFORE the skin incision/start, the ENTIRE TEAM STOPS all non-essential activity and ACTIVELY, VERBALLY confirms, at minimum: correct PATIENT (two identifiers), correct PROCEDURE, correct SITE/SIDE and patient POSITION, that the CONSENT is correct, that the correct IMPLANTS/equipment/imaging are available, that prophylactic ANTIBIOTICS were given (if indicated), and any anticipated critical concerns; EVERY team member PARTICIPATES and must AGREE before the procedure begins, and the time-out is DOCUMENTED. The time-out is performed for EVERY procedure (and a new time-out is done if the procedure/site changes or a second procedure is added). THIS PATIENT: verification caught and reconciled the schedule discrepancy; the surgeon marked the RIGHT knee with the awake patient confirming; and the full-team time-out before incision verbally confirmed patient/procedure/RIGHT-knee site/position/consent/implants/antibiotic.
labs.r: Universal Protocol = (1) VERIFY (2 identifiers; correct patient/procedure/site; consent matches; imaging/implants present; involve the patient; RECONCILE discrepancies before proceeding); (2) MARK the site by the operating provider, unambiguous, visible after prep/drape, only the operative site (esp. laterality/multiple levels); (3) TIME-OUT immediately before incision — whole team stops + verbally confirms patient/procedure/site/position/consent/implants/antibiotic, all agree, documented. RN role: verify + ensure marking + lead/participate in time-out.

labs.n: WHO Surgical Safety Checklist + speak-up culture
labs.v: THE WHO SURGICAL SAFETY CHECKLIST structures three team pauses around the operation and reduces surgical mortality/complications: (1) SIGN IN — BEFORE INDUCTION OF ANESTHESIA: confirm patient identity, site, procedure, and consent; site marked; anesthesia machine/medication safety check complete; pulse oximeter on; known ALLERGIES reviewed; difficult-AIRWAY/aspiration risk assessed (and plan/equipment ready); risk of significant BLOOD LOSS assessed (IV access/blood available). (2) TIME OUT — BEFORE SKIN INCISION: all team members INTRODUCE themselves by name and role; the team verbally confirms the correct patient, site, and procedure (the Universal Protocol time-out); confirms ANTIBIOTIC prophylaxis was given within the last 60 minutes (if indicated); anticipates CRITICAL EVENTS (surgeon reviews critical/unexpected steps, expected blood loss; anesthesia reviews patient-specific concerns; nursing confirms sterility, equipment, and any issues); and confirms essential IMAGING is displayed. (3) SIGN OUT — BEFORE THE PATIENT LEAVES THE ROOM: the team confirms the name of the recorded procedure; that the instrument, SPONGE, and NEEDLE COUNTS are CORRECT; that any SPECIMENS are correctly LABELED (patient + site); whether there were any EQUIPMENT problems to address; and the key concerns for the patient's RECOVERY and management. SPEAK-UP / 'STOP-THE-LINE' CULTURE underlies the entire checklist: ANY team member — including the circulating nurse, scrub tech, or a student — can and MUST HALT the procedure if something does not match, a count is off, sterility is breached, or anything seems unsafe, WITHOUT fear of reprisal. This requires a FLATTENED HIERARCHY and a JUST CULTURE (focus on system safety, not blame), and it is one of the most powerful safety mechanisms in the OR; the checklist + a real speak-up culture, used together with full team engagement (not as a rote box-ticking exercise), prevent errors. THIS PATIENT'S team completed the sign-in, a participatory time-out, and the sign-out (procedure recorded, counts correct, specimens labeled), within a culture where the nurse was empowered to stop the line.
labs.r: WHO Surgical Safety Checklist: SIGN IN (before anesthesia — ID/site/procedure/consent, anesthesia/airway/allergy/blood-loss checks), TIME OUT (before incision — introductions + verify patient/site/procedure + antibiotic + critical events + imaging), SIGN OUT (before leaving — procedure recorded, COUNTS correct, specimens labeled, equipment, recovery concerns). SPEAK-UP/stop-the-line: ANY member can halt for any concern (flattened hierarchy + just culture). RN role: full team-engaged checklist + empowered to stop the line.

labs.v: SURGICAL COUNTS prevent RETAINED SURGICAL ITEMS (a never event — most commonly a SPONGE, also needles/sharps and instruments): SPONGES, SHARPS (needles, blades), and INSTRUMENTS are COUNTED — typically by TWO people together (the scrub nurse/tech and the circulating nurse) — at defined points: a BASELINE count before the procedure, BEFORE CLOSURE of a cavity/wound, at SKIN CLOSURE (final count), and whenever scrub or circulating staff are PERMANENTLY RELIEVED. If a COUNT IS INCORRECT: notify the team/SURGEON immediately, the surgeon explores the wound, the team SEARCHES the field/floor/trash/drapes, and an intraoperative/postoperative X-RAY is obtained to locate the radiopaque item — the discrepancy must be RECONCILED (item found and removed, or definitively accounted for) BEFORE the patient leaves the OR; all counts and actions are DOCUMENTED. STERILE TECHNIQUE / SURGICAL ASEPSIS maintains the STERILE FIELD: only STERILE items contact sterile items ('sterile-to-sterile'); the sterile field is established as close as possible to the time of use, kept in CONSTANT VIEW, and NEVER left unattended (an unattended field is considered contaminated); personnel do NOT reach OVER the field or turn their BACK to it, and keep their hands above waist/table level and in front; a sterile GOWN is sterile in FRONT from chest to the level of the sterile field and the sleeves to 2 inches above the elbow (the back, below the field, and axillae are NOT sterile); the OUTER 1 INCH (2.5 cm) of a sterile drape/wrapper edge is NON-sterile; MOISTURE wicking through (strikethrough) contaminates; sterile packages are opened away from the body with the first flap opened away; and the cardinal rule — 'WHEN IN DOUBT, consider it CONTAMINATED' (discard/replace any item of questionable sterility). Any BREAK in sterile technique is corrected immediately (replace the item/re-drape/re-glove). THESE measures, plus correct counts, protect the patient from retained items and from contamination. THIS PATIENT: a correct baseline count, intra-op count tracking, and correct final counts at closure (with the contingency plan—notify/search/X-ray—ready if a count were off); the sterile field was maintained with sterile-to-sterile technique and edges treated as non-sterile.
labs.r: COUNTS (sponges/sharps/instruments) by TWO people at baseline, before closure, at skin closure, and at staff relief prevent RETAINED items; an INCORRECT count → notify surgeon + search + X-RAY + reconcile before the patient leaves. STERILE technique: sterile-to-sterile; keep field in view/never unattended; don't reach over/turn back; gown sterile front chest-to-field + sleeves; outer 1 inch non-sterile; moisture = strikethrough contamination; 'when in doubt, contaminated.' RN role: counts + protect the sterile field.

labs.n: SSI-prevention bundle, fire/positioning safety; Pertinent NEGATIVES + protective factors
labs.v: SURGICAL-SITE-INFECTION (SSI) PREVENTION BUNDLE: (1) prophylactic ANTIBIOTICS administered within 60 MINUTES BEFORE incision (within 120 min for vancomycin/fluoroquinolones), with intraoperative RE-DOSING for long procedures or major blood loss and appropriate DISCONTINUATION postoperatively (usually within 24 h); (2) appropriate HAIR REMOVAL — do NOT shave with a razor (razor microabrasions raise SSI risk); use electric CLIPPERS or do not remove hair; (3) skin ANTISEPSIS with an appropriate agent (commonly chlorhexidine-alcohol) applied correctly and ALLOWED TO DRY; (4) maintain perioperative NORMOTHERMIA (active warming — hypothermia impairs immunity/healing and increases SSI); (5) GLUCOSE control (perioperative hyperglycemia increases SSI, even in non-diabetics); (6) maintain meticulous STERILE technique + appropriate tissue oxygenation. OTHER PERIOPERATIVE SAFETY: LABEL all medications and solutions on AND off the sterile field (unlabeled syringes are a serious error source); correctly HANDLE and LABEL SPECIMENS (patient + site + type); FIRE SAFETY — the surgical FIRE TRIAD is an OXIDIZER (supplemental O2/nitrous), an IGNITION source (electrocautery/laser), and FUEL (alcohol-based prep, drapes, hair) → allow alcohol preps to FULLY DRY, manage oxygen, and communicate before using cautery near oxygen; POSITIONING with PRESSURE-INJURY and nerve-injury prevention (padding, safe positioning, securing); VTE prophylaxis; and verifying correct implants/equipment. PERTINENT NEGATIVES (correct safety practice): the team did NOT proceed through the schedule/consent discrepancy (it was reconciled to RIGHT knee BEFORE marking/incision — preventing a wrong-site surgery); the site was marked by the SURGEON with the patient involved (NOT by ancillary staff, NOT on the wrong/both sides); the time-out was an ACTIVE whole-team verbal verification (NOT skipped or done by one person); counts were performed by two people and reconciled (NOT skipped); a RAZOR was NOT used for hair removal (clippers); the antibiotic was timed within 60 minutes (NOT given hours early/late or forgotten); the alcohol prep was allowed to DRY before cautery (fire prevention); and the sterile field was not left unattended/contaminated. PROTECTIVE FACTORS: a strong safety culture with the Universal Protocol + WHO checklist used with full team engagement; an empowered circulating-nurse advocate who caught the near-miss and could STOP THE LINE; accurate two-person counts; sterile technique; the SSI bundle (timely antibiotic, clippers, antisepsis, normothermia, glucose); fire/positioning safeguards; and thorough documentation + SBAR hand-off. EXPECTED outcome (as here): the correct procedure on the correct site, no retained items, no surgical fire or positioning injury, and reduced SSI risk — a safe operation. The case underscores that surgical safety is a TEAM, PROCESS-DRIVEN endeavor in which the nurse's verification, time-out leadership, counts, asepsis, and willingness to speak up prevent catastrophic, preventable harm. NURSING ROLE: execute the Universal Protocol + WHO checklist with the team, reconcile discrepancies, manage counts, maintain asepsis, deliver the SSI bundle, ensure fire/positioning/specimen safety, empower speak-up, and document.
labs.r: SSI bundle: prophylactic antibiotic within 60 min of incision (re-dose/discontinue appropriately); CLIPPERS not razor; skin antisepsis (let dry); NORMOTHERMIA; glucose control; sterile technique. Plus: label all meds on/off field, label specimens, FIRE safety (triad: O2 + cautery + alcohol prep → let prep dry), positioning/pressure-nerve protection, VTE prophylaxis. Negatives confirm correct practice (reconciled discrepancy, surgeon-marked site, active team time-out, two-person counts, clippers, timed antibiotic, dried prep). RN role: SSI bundle + perioperative safety + speak-up + documentation.

nursesNote: Pre-op holding → OR — 68 y/o woman K. Olsen for elective RIGHT total knee arthroplasty (end-stage OA). Perioperative (circulating) RN executing surgical-safety processes to prevent NEVER EVENTS (wrong-site/procedure/patient surgery; retained surgical items) + SSI. PRE-PROCEDURE VERIFICATION (holding): TWO identifiers (name + DOB); confirmed procedure + SITE/SIDE (RIGHT knee) with the AWAKE patient; reviewed consent; NEAR-MISS CAUGHT — OR schedule listed 'left knee' while consent, surgeon's note, imaging, and patient all state RIGHT → flagged + RECONCILED (corrected) BEFORE proceeding (did NOT assume/proceed through the mismatch). SITE MARKING: SURGEON marked the RIGHT knee (initials) with the patient confirming; marked ONLY the operative site. WHO SIGN-IN before anesthesia (ID/site/procedure/consent + anesthesia/airway/allergy/blood-loss checks). SSI BUNDLE: prophylactic ANTIBIOTIC given within 60 min before incision; CLIPPERS (not a razor) for hair removal; chlorhexidine-alcohol prep allowed to DRY (fire safety — O2 + cautery + alcohol triad); NORMOTHERMIA maintained (active warming); glucose monitored. TIME-OUT immediately before incision: ENTIRE team STOPPED + verbally confirmed correct PATIENT (2 identifiers), PROCEDURE, SITE/SIDE (RIGHT, marked + visible), position, consent, implant/equipment availability, antibiotic-within-60-min, imaging displayed; all agreed; introductions done; documented. COUNTS: baseline sponge/sharp/instrument count (two-person: scrub + circulating) correct; tracked intra-op. STERILE TECHNIQUE: sterile-to-sterile; field kept in view + never left unattended; no reaching over/turning back; outer 1 inch treated as non-sterile; 'when in doubt, contaminated'; meds on field labeled. SPEAK-UP/stop-the-line culture active (any member may halt). CLOSURE/SIGN-OUT: counts repeated before closure + at skin closure = CORRECT; WHO SIGN-OUT (procedure recorded, counts correct, specimens labeled by patient+site, equipment issues, recovery concerns); contingency if a count were INCORRECT → notify surgeon + search field/floor/trash + X-RAY + reconcile BEFORE patient leaves OR. PACU: correct RIGHT-knee arthroplasty completed, NO wrong-site error, NO retained item, NO surgical fire/positioning injury; SSI-prevention delivered; SBAR hand-off; documentation complete; family updated. NEGATIVES (correct practice): didn't proceed through the discrepancy; surgeon (not ancillary) marked only the correct site; active whole-team time-out (not skipped/solo); two-person counts reconciled; clippers not razor; antibiotic timed; prep dried before cautery; sterile field protected. Surgical safety = a TEAM, PROCESS-DRIVEN effort; the nurse's verification + time-out + counts + asepsis + speak-up prevent catastrophic preventable harm.
providerOrders: PERIOPERATIVE SAFETY: elective right total knee arthroplasty — apply the Universal Protocol + WHO Surgical Safety Checklist; prevent wrong-site/procedure/patient surgery, retained surgical items, and SSI
DIAGNOSIS: end-stage right knee osteoarthritis for total knee arthroplasty; perioperative safety focus
CODE STATUS: full code; alert with capacity; consented
PRE-PROCEDURE VERIFICATION:

Confirm correct patient with TWO identifiers (name + DOB/MRN — not room number); confirm correct procedure and site/side; verify consent matches; ensure imaging/labs/implants/equipment available; involve the awake patient
Reconcile ANY discrepancy (consent vs schedule vs surgeon vs imaging vs patient) BEFORE proceeding — stop and clarify; do not assume
SITE MARKING:

Surgeon (operating provider) marks the operative site with the patient involved; unambiguous mark (initials) at/near incision, visible after prep/drape; mark only the operative site (required for laterality/multiple structures/levels)
WHO SURGICAL SAFETY CHECKLIST:
SIGN IN (before anesthesia): identity/site/procedure/consent, anesthesia + airway/aspiration + allergy + blood-loss checks, pulse oximeter on

TIME OUT (before incision): whole team stops; introductions; verbally confirm correct patient (2 identifiers)/procedure/site-side/position/consent/implants-equipment/imaging; confirm prophylactic antibiotic given within 60 min; anticipate critical events; all members participate and agree; document
SIGN OUT (before leaving room): confirm procedure recorded, counts correct, specimens labeled (patient + site), equipment issues, recovery concerns
SURGICAL COUNTS (prevent retained items):
Count sponges, sharps, and instruments (two-person: scrub + circulating) at baseline, before cavity/wound closure, at skin closure, and at permanent staff relief
If a count is incorrect: notify the surgeon, search field/floor/trash/drapes, obtain X-ray to locate the radiopaque item, and reconcile BEFORE the patient leaves the OR; document
STERILE TECHNIQUE / SURGICAL ASEPSIS:

Maintain the sterile field (sterile-to-sterile); keep in view + never leave unattended; do not reach over/turn back; treat the outer 1 inch as non-sterile; moisture = strikethrough contamination; 'when in doubt, contaminated' — replace any questionable item
Label all medications/solutions on and off the sterile field
SSI-PREVENTION BUNDLE:
Prophylactic antibiotic within 60 minutes before incision (within 120 min for vancomycin/fluoroquinolone); re-dose for long cases/major blood loss; discontinue appropriately postoperatively
Hair removal with clippers or none (NOT a razor); appropriate skin antisepsis (e.g., chlorhexidine-alcohol) allowed to dry; maintain normothermia; control glucose; appropriate oxygenation
FIRE + POSITIONING + SPECIMEN SAFETY:
Fire safety: allow alcohol prep to fully dry before cautery; manage oxidizer (O2)/ignition (cautery)/fuel (prep, drapes) triad; communicate before cautery near oxygen
Safe positioning with pressure-injury/nerve-injury prevention (padding); VTE prophylaxis; correctly handle/label specimens; verify implants/equipment
TEAM CULTURE + DOCUMENTATION:
Empower a speak-up / stop-the-line culture — any team member may halt the procedure for any safety concern (flattened hierarchy, just culture)
Document verification, marking, time-out/checklist, counts, antibiotic timing, and SBAR hand-off to PACU

steps.title: Recognize Cues
steps.inst: Select ALL considerations that are critical for safe surgery.

opts.text: Wrong-site/procedure/patient surgery and retained surgical items are preventable NEVER EVENTS prevented by the Universal Protocol, the WHO checklist, counts, and team verification
opts.rat: PRIORITY CUE — recognizing these never events frames the whole safety process. RN role: execute the safety processes.

opts.text: A discrepancy between the OR schedule ('left knee') and the consent/surgeon/imaging/patient (RIGHT knee) must be reconciled BEFORE proceeding — never assume or proceed through a mismatch
opts.rat: PRIORITY CUE — catching/reconciling the discrepancy prevents a wrong-site surgery. RN role: verify + reconcile before proceeding.

opts.text: Verify the correct patient with two identifiers, confirm procedure/site/side and consent, and involve the awake patient; the surgeon marks the operative site
opts.rat: PRIORITY CUE — verification + site marking are core Universal Protocol steps. RN role: two-identifier verification + ensure marking.

opts.text: Perform a whole-team TIME-OUT immediately before incision (all stop and verbally confirm patient/procedure/site/position/consent/implants/antibiotic), with every member participating
opts.rat: PRIORITY CUE — the active team time-out is the final barrier to wrong-site surgery. RN role: lead/participate in the time-out.

opts.text: Count sponges, sharps, and instruments (two-person) at baseline, before closure, and at skin closure; reconcile any incorrect count (notify surgeon + search + X-ray) before the patient leaves
opts.rat: PRIORITY CUE — counts prevent retained items. RN role: counts + reconciliation.

opts.text: Maintain sterile technique/asepsis and deliver the SSI-prevention bundle (antibiotic within 60 min, clippers not razor, antisepsis allowed to dry, normothermia, glucose control)
opts.rat: PRIORITY CUE — asepsis + the SSI bundle prevent infection. RN role: sterile field + SSI bundle.

opts.text: Maintain a speak-up / stop-the-line culture (any team member can halt for a concern) plus fire, positioning, and specimen safety
opts.rat: PRIORITY CUE — speak-up culture + fire/positioning/specimen safety prevent harm. RN role: empower speak-up + manage these safeguards.

opts.text: Allergies: NKDA — document and proceed (relevant for prophylactic antibiotic + latex)
opts.rat: Allergy documentation matters before antibiotics/latex exposure. Relevant but not the central cue.

opts.text: Since the surgeon is confident it's the left knee per the schedule, proceed with marking and incision on the left without resolving the discrepancy, to avoid delaying the case
opts.rat: ⚠️ WRONG + DANGEROUS — this would cause a wrong-site surgery. Any discrepancy must be reconciled to the source of truth (consent/surgeon's plan/imaging/patient = RIGHT) BEFORE proceeding; never proceed through a mismatch to save time.

opts.text: Skip the time-out and surgical counts for this 'routine' case to keep the OR on schedule
opts.rat: ⚠️ WRONG. The time-out and counts are mandatory for EVERY case regardless of how routine; skipping them is exactly how wrong-site surgery and retained items occur.

opts.text: The Universal Protocol = pre-procedure verification (two identifiers, correct patient/procedure/site, consent, reconcile discrepancies) + site marking by the operating provider + a whole-team time-out before incision
opts.rat: CORRECT — the Universal Protocol framework. Foundation for preventing wrong-site surgery.

opts.text: The site is marked by the licensed provider performing the procedure, with the patient involved, only at the operative site, and visible after prep/draping
opts.rat: CORRECT — site-marking principle. Foundation for laterality/level safety.

opts.text: The WHO checklist has three pauses — sign in (before anesthesia), time out (before incision), sign out (before leaving) — and the time-out requires active participation by every team member
opts.rat: CORRECT — WHO checklist structure. Foundation for team verification.

opts.text: Surgical counts (sponges/sharps/instruments) by two people prevent retained items; an incorrect count requires notifying the surgeon, searching, and an X-ray, reconciled before the patient leaves
opts.rat: CORRECT — counts principle. Foundation for preventing retained items.

opts.text: Sterile technique: sterile-to-sterile, keep the field in view/never unattended, don't reach over or turn your back, the outer 1 inch is non-sterile, moisture contaminates, and 'when in doubt, contaminated'
opts.rat: CORRECT — surgical asepsis principles. Foundation for preventing contamination.

opts.text: SSI prevention: prophylactic antibiotic within 60 minutes of incision, clippers (not a razor) for hair, skin antisepsis allowed to dry, normothermia, and glucose control
opts.rat: CORRECT — SSI bundle principle. Foundation for infection prevention.

opts.text: Hair at the surgical site should be shaved with a razor the night before to ensure a clean field
opts.rat: ⚠️ WRONG. Razor shaving causes microabrasions that INCREASE SSI risk; use clippers or no hair removal, ideally close to the time of surgery.

opts.text: Only the surgeon and anesthesiologist need to participate in the time-out; the circulating nurse can keep working to save time
opts.rat: ⚠️ WRONG. The time-out requires the ENTIRE team to stop and actively participate (including the circulating and scrub nurses); partial participation defeats its purpose.

opts.text: Verify correct patient/procedure/site and RECONCILE the schedule-vs-consent discrepancy before anything else proceeds (prevent wrong-site surgery)
opts.rat: #1 — HIGHEST. Resolving the site discrepancy + verification prevents a catastrophic wrong-site surgery and gates everything downstream. RN role: verify + reconcile.

opts.text: Ensure site marking by the surgeon and a whole-team time-out (active verbal verification) immediately before incision
opts.rat: #2 — HIGH. Marking + the team time-out are the key final barriers to wrong-site surgery. RN role: marking + time-out.

opts.text: Perform and reconcile surgical counts and maintain the sterile field to prevent retained items and contamination
opts.rat: #3 — HIGH. Counts + asepsis prevent retained items and SSI. RN role: counts + sterile technique.

opts.text: Deliver the SSI-prevention bundle (timely antibiotic, clippers, antisepsis, normothermia, glucose) and fire/positioning safety
opts.rat: #4 — Significant. The SSI bundle + fire/positioning safety prevent infection and other harm. RN role: SSI bundle + safeguards.

opts.text: Maintain a speak-up culture, complete the WHO sign-out, manage specimens, and hand off via SBAR with documentation
opts.rat: #5 — Important + ongoing. Speak-up + sign-out + specimens + hand-off/documentation complete safe care. RN role: speak-up + sign-out + hand-off.

steps.title: Generate Solutions
steps.inst: For EACH action, classify as INDICATED or NOT INDICATED for safe surgery.

opts.text: Verify the patient with two identifiers, confirm procedure/site/side and consent with the awake patient, and reconcile the schedule-vs-consent discrepancy to the RIGHT knee before proceeding
opts.rat: Core verification + discrepancy reconciliation. RN role: verify + reconcile.

opts.text: Have the surgeon mark the correct operative site (initials) with the patient involved, visible after prep/draping
opts.rat: Correct site marking by the operating provider. RN role: ensure marking.

opts.text: Conduct a whole-team time-out before incision (all stop, verbally confirm patient/procedure/site/position/consent/implants/antibiotic) and the WHO sign-in/sign-out
opts.rat: Active team verification at each WHO pause. RN role: lead/participate in time-out + checklist.

opts.text: Perform two-person counts of sponges/sharps/instruments at the required points and reconcile any incorrect count (notify surgeon + search + X-ray) before the patient leaves
opts.rat: Prevents retained items. RN role: counts + reconciliation.

opts.text: Maintain sterile technique (sterile-to-sterile, field in view, treat edges as non-sterile, when-in-doubt-contaminated) and label all medications on/off the field
opts.rat: Surgical asepsis + medication-labeling safety. RN role: protect the sterile field + label meds.

opts.text: Give the prophylactic antibiotic within 60 minutes before incision, use clippers (not a razor), allow the antiseptic prep to dry, and maintain normothermia + glucose control
opts.rat: SSI-prevention bundle + fire safety (dry prep). RN role: SSI bundle.

opts.text: Empower any team member to stop the line for a safety concern, complete the sign-out (counts/specimens), and hand off via SBAR
opts.rat: Speak-up culture + sign-out + safe hand-off. RN role: speak-up + sign-out + hand-off.

opts.text: Proceed with surgery on the side listed on the schedule without resolving the discrepancy, to avoid delaying the case
opts.rat: ⚠️ WRONG/DANGEROUS — proceeding through an unresolved discrepancy risks wrong-site surgery. Reconcile to the source of truth before proceeding.

opts.text: Skip the time-out and surgical counts because the case is routine and the team is experienced
opts.rat: ⚠️ WRONG — time-out and counts are mandatory for EVERY case; skipping them causes wrong-site surgery and retained items.

opts.text: Shave the surgical site with a razor and start the incision immediately after applying the alcohol-based prep (still wet)
opts.rat: ⚠️ WRONG — razor shaving increases SSI, and incising/using cautery before an alcohol prep dries is a surgical-fire hazard. Use clippers and let the prep dry.

opts.text: Leave the sterile field unattended and reach across it to retrieve supplies during the case
opts.rat: ⚠️ WRONG — an unattended field is considered contaminated, and reaching over the field contaminates it. Maintain the field in view and never reach over it.

steps.title: Take Action
steps.inst: Rank the order of the perioperative nurse's actions. #1 = FIRST.

opts.text: Pre-procedure verification with two identifiers + confirm procedure/site/consent + reconcile the schedule-vs-consent discrepancy (to the RIGHT knee)
opts.rat: #1 — FIRST. Establish the correct patient/procedure/site and resolve the discrepancy before anything proceeds. RN role: verify + reconcile.

opts.text: Ensure the surgeon marks the correct site and complete the WHO sign-in before anesthesia
opts.rat: #2 — Second. Marking + sign-in precede anesthesia/incision. RN role: marking + sign-in.

opts.text: Lead/participate in the whole-team time-out immediately before incision (and ensure the antibiotic was given within 60 minutes); complete the baseline count
opts.rat: #3 — Third. The time-out + baseline count + antibiotic timing immediately precede incision. RN role: time-out + count + antibiotic.

opts.text: Maintain sterile technique + the SSI bundle intra-op and track counts; empower speak-up
opts.rat: #4 — Intra-op. Asepsis + SSI measures + counts + speak-up during the case. RN role: asepsis + SSI + counts.

opts.text: Perform final counts, complete the WHO sign-out (counts/specimens), and hand off to PACU via SBAR with documentation
opts.rat: #5 — End/after. Final counts + sign-out + safe hand-off + documentation. RN role: sign-out + hand-off + documentation.

steps.title: Evaluate Outcomes
steps.inst: For each action taken, classify as Appropriate or Inappropriate.

opts.text: The nurse verified two identifiers, caught the schedule-vs-consent 'left vs right' discrepancy, and reconciled it to the RIGHT knee before any marking or incision
opts.rat: Correct verification + discrepancy reconciliation that prevented a wrong-site surgery. Model handling.

opts.text: The surgeon marked the right knee with the awake patient confirming, and a whole-team time-out verbally verified everything before incision
opts.rat: Correct site marking + active team time-out. Model handling.

opts.text: Two-person sponge/sharp/instrument counts were performed at the required points and were correct at closure, with a plan to search + X-ray if any count were off
opts.rat: Correct counts + reconciliation plan to prevent retained items. Model handling.

opts.text: The SSI bundle was delivered (antibiotic within 60 min, clippers, antiseptic allowed to dry, normothermia, glucose control), and the field was kept sterile
opts.rat: Correct SSI prevention + asepsis. Model handling.

opts.text: To stay on schedule, the team proceeded on the side listed on the OR schedule without resolving the documented discrepancy
opts.rat: ⚠️ Proceeding through an unresolved site discrepancy risks wrong-site surgery — a never event. Must reconcile first. Incorrect.

opts.text: The time-out and surgical counts were skipped because the case was considered routine and the team experienced
opts.rat: ⚠️ Time-out and counts are mandatory for every case; skipping them causes wrong-site surgery and retained items. Incorrect.

opts.text: The surgical site was shaved with a razor and the incision was started while the alcohol prep was still wet
opts.rat: ⚠️ Razor shaving increases SSI, and a wet alcohol prep + cautery is a surgical-fire hazard. Incorrect — use clippers and let the prep dry.

opts.text: The circulating nurse, noticing a possible break in sterility, stopped the line and the item was replaced before continuing
opts.rat: Speak-up/stop-the-line for a sterility concern + correcting the break. Model handling of a just, safety-first culture.

opts.text: At sign-out the team confirmed the recorded procedure, correct counts, and correctly labeled specimens, then handed off to PACU via SBAR
opts.rat: Correct WHO sign-out + specimen labeling + structured hand-off. Model handling.

opts.text: An incorrect final sponge count was noted, so the team simply documented it and sent the patient to PACU to avoid delay
opts.rat: ⚠️ An incorrect count must be reconciled (notify surgeon, search, X-ray) BEFORE the patient leaves the OR — ignoring it risks a retained item (never event). Incorrect.
